commit.message:
Add perceptual loss (LPIPS) on VAE-decoded latent crops

Implements periodic LPIPS perceptual loss computed on random spatial crops
of predicted latents decoded through a frozen VAE decoder. Key design:

- PerceptualLossModule: lazily loads VAE decoder (fp16, frozen) and
  LPIPS(AlexNet, fp32, frozen) on first use (~110MB total)
- Random latent crop (default 32x32 → 256px decoded) keeps memory
  constant regardless of training resolution (~325MB overhead)
- x0 reconstruction: x0_hat = zt - t * v_pred, with gradient flow
  through VAE decode for the prediction
- Computed every N micro-steps (default 4) to amortize decode cost
- Disabled by default; enable with perceptual.enabled: true in config

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix stack overflow in AI destructor chain: use qualified ~AIModule() calls

Root cause: Classes like ConstructionAIModule, DiplomacyAIModule,
EmotionalAIModule, ResearchAIModule, and ResourceAIModule share
AIModule's memory layout via padding fields but don't inherit from it.
Their destructors called ((AIModule*)this)->~AIModule() which is a
VIRTUAL call through the vtable — dispatching back to the current
class's destructor, causing infinite recursion and stack overflow.

Fix: Use qualified calls (AIModule::~AIModule()) to bypass virtual
dispatch. Same fix applied to ConstructionItem::~ConstructionItem()
which had identical issue with BaseItem::~BaseItem().

Also removed temporary SEH wrappers from TribeMainDecisionAIModule
that were added during investigation.

Verified: 3 clean shutdown cycles with ExitCode=0, no exceptions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

transport: implement IOUSBHost device discovery and session scaffold (#475)

Replace IOUSBHost transport stubs with real implementations using Apple's
IOUSBHost and IOKit frameworks (macOS 15+):

- IOUSBHostTransport: IOKit matching for device discovery by VID:PID and
  MTP interface class (6/1/1), with vendor-specific fallback for Android
- IOUSBHostLink: full USB lifecycle (claim interface, open bulk/interrupt
  pipes via IOUSBHostPipe, release on close)
- Bulk transfer helpers: sendIORequest-based bulk read/write with timeouts
- PTP container encoding/decoding for command/data/response phases
- Session management: openSession, closeSession, getDeviceInfo, getStorageIDs,
  deleteObject via PTP command execution
- IOUSBHostDeviceLocator: real IOKit enumeration of MTP-capable USB devices
  with known-vendor heuristics (Google, Samsung, Xiaomi, OnePlus, etc.)
- Comprehensive error types: deviceNotFound, claimFailed, noMTPInterface,
  endpointNotFound, ioError, invalidState, pipeStall, transferTimeout
- 19 new tests covering error types, default link behavior, factory,
  stub methods, PTP encoding, device locator, and event stream

Remaining stubs (TODO): getStorageInfo, getObjectHandles, getObjectInfos,
resetDevice, moveObject, copyObject, data-out phase, hotplug monitoring.

feat: two-tier channels — community (unverified) vs verified

Agents can freely create channels via create_channel — they start as
community channels (verified: false). A channel becomes verified only
when an admin creates a matching GitHub Discussions category.

Changes:
- channel.py: new channels default to verified: false (was hardcoded true)
- reconcile_channels.py: auto-detects which channels have a matching
  GitHub Discussions category and flips verified accordingly
- CONSTITUTION.md: documented the two-tier system (Community vs Verified)

Verification is provable — the GitHub category either exists or it doesn't.
No subjective approval, just infrastructure.
